# About Korbey Lague PLLP

## Identity

Korbey Lague PLLP is a CPA firm located at 1 Pondview Pl, Tyngsborough, MA 01879. The firm can be reached by phone at (978) 649-2155, by fax at (978) 649-2160, or by email at info@korbeylague.com. Their website is [korbeylague.com](https://www.korbeylague.com).

The firm operates with a team of nine, including two credentialed partners:

- **Kelsey Korbey, CPA** — Partner
- **Ron Lague, CPA, PFS** — Partner (PFS is the Personal Financial Specialist designation awarded by the AICPA)
- **Tanya Lombardi** — Office Manager
- **Jackie Estes, MBA**
- **Mike Riordan, MBA**
- **Richard DelGaudio, CPA**
- **Kristine Ciccarelli**
- **Ryan Lague**
- **Scott Denisevich**

Office hours during standard season are Monday through Thursday, 9:00 AM–3:00 PM, and Friday by appointment. During tax season (February 1 through April 15), hours expand to Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM–5:00 PM, and Saturday, 9:00 AM–Noon.

The firm maintains a presence on Facebook and Yelp, and holds an affiliation with Sage Intacct as a technology partner. Ron Lague's PFS credential implies AICPA affiliation.

---

## Positioning & Differentiation

Korbey Lague PLLP positions itself as a year-round accounting partner — not a firm that disappears after April 15. Their positioning statement is direct: *"Your Business Deserves More Than Tax Season."* The firm's tagline is **"Inspired by your success."**

Key differentiators include:

- **Year-round advisory availability** — the firm emphasizes being present in July, not just April, framing ongoing engagement as the default rather than a premium add-on.
- **Tiered bookkeeping packages** — Basic, Standard, and Complete tiers allow businesses at different stages to access structured, scalable support.
- **Virtual CFO services** — described as delivering CFO-level financial insight at a fraction of the cost of a full-time hire, giving small and mid-sized businesses access to monthly financial clarity and strategic guidance.
- **Sage Intacct partnership** — the firm uses Sage Intacct as part of its accounting system setup and financial reporting capabilities, signaling a commitment to cloud-based, business-intelligence-oriented tooling.
- **Multi-credentialed team** — two CPA partners, one of whom holds the AICPA's PFS (Personal Financial Specialist) designation, plus two MBA-credentialed staff members.

The firm serves both individuals and businesses across a wide range of industries, with particular depth in healthcare, trades, nonprofits, service businesses, and startups.

---

## Industries Served

### Healthcare Professionals

**Who they are:** Physicians, dentists, therapists, and specialists — solo practices and small group practices.

**Client pain point:** *"I just opened my practice and I need someone who actually knows how medical practices work, not just someone who handles tax season."*

**Value proposition:** Healthcare professionals need an accounting partner who understands the financial structure of a medical or dental practice — not a generalist who treats them like any other small business. Korbey Lague offers industry-aware tax, bookkeeping, and advisory services for this client type.

---

### Contractors & Trades

**Who they are:** HVAC, electrical, plumbing, general contractors, landscaping — often owner-operated businesses.

**Client pain point:** *"My buddy said I should stop doing my own books. I've got 8 guys now and no idea if I'm profitable."*

**Value proposition:** Trades businesses that have outgrown DIY bookkeeping need structured financial visibility. The firm's tiered bookkeeping packages, payroll services (including contractor and 1099 distinctions), and advisory support address the profitability and compliance gaps common at this growth stage.

---

### Nonprofits

**Who they are:** 501(c)(3) organizations — social services, arts, education, faith-based, and community foundations.

**Client pain point:** *"We have a grant audit coming up and our books are a mess. We need someone who actually understands how nonprofits work."*

**Value proposition:** The firm offers dedicated nonprofit accounting services, including fund accounting, Form 990 preparation, and grant compliance support — areas that require specialized knowledge distinct from standard business accounting.

---

### Service-Based Businesses

**Who they are:** Marketing agencies, professional services firms, cleaning and maintenance companies, salons, and staffing firms.

**Client pain point:** *"I had a great year but somehow I owe $30K in taxes. I need someone proactive, not just reactive."*

**Value proposition:** Service businesses with strong revenue but poor tax planning visibility benefit from year-round advisory engagement, proactive tax strategy, and monthly financial reporting — all services the firm offers as part of its core model.

---

### Business Startups

**Who they are:** New LLCs and S-corps across industries — often individuals transitioning from employee to business owner.

**Client pain point:** *"I'm starting a business and I don't even know what questions to ask. I just know I need a CPA."*

**Value proposition:** The firm's Business Foundation Services are designed for new owners who need entity type analysis, accounting system setup, and foundational bookkeeping — a structured on-ramp for businesses that are starting from zero.

---

## About

### Our Story
URL: /about/our-story

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->

## Where It Started

Most small business owners have the same story: they find a CPA, hand over their documents in March, get a return filed in April, and don't hear from anyone until the following winter. That's not a relationship. That's a transaction with a 12-month gap in the middle.

Korbey Lague PLLP was founded in Tyngsborough because Kelsey Korbey, CPA and Ron Lague, CPA, PFS had watched that pattern play out too many times — and knew there was a better way to serve the people building businesses and careers in Massachusetts. The gap between tax seasons isn't downtime. It's when your decisions about hiring, cash flow, debt, and growth actually happen. A firm that only shows up for the filing isn't present for any of it.

The founding idea was direct: build a firm where the relationship doesn't go quiet on April 16th. Year-round advisory work, not seasonal compliance. That conviction is still the engine behind everything Korbey Lague does today.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: tyngsborough-community-roots.jpg | query: "small town New England community street" -->

## Why Tyngsborough — and Why It Matters

This firm didn't land in Tyngsborough by accident. It was a deliberate choice to put down roots in a community where business owners know each other by name — where the contractor who builds your addition also uses your accountant, and word travels fast if you're not doing right by people.

That accountability isn't uncomfortable. It's the point. Being local means the firm's reputation lives or dies on what it does for its clients every month of the year, not just during tax season. It means Kelsey and Ron aren't abstract names on a letterhead — they're neighbors. That proximity sharpens attention and deepens commitment in ways that a regional office or a national chain simply can't replicate. Choosing Tyngsborough was a statement about what kind of firm this would be.

<!-- block: industry-cards | variant: 3-col -->

## The Clients Who Shaped Us

The firm didn't build its expertise in a classroom. It was built in real conversations with clients who needed more than a filed return.

### Healthcare Professionals
icon: Stethoscope

Physicians, dentists, and private practice owners brought complexity: entity structuring, compensation planning, and the particular financial pressure of running a clinical business while managing patient care. These clients pushed the firm to go deep on practice economics, not just tax compliance.

### Contractors and Trades
icon: Hammer

Contractors taught the firm about cash flow timing, job costing, bonding capacity, and the difference between a profitable project on paper and one that actually pays. The relationship goes year-round because the financial decisions do too.

### Nonprofits
icon: Users

Nonprofit clients demanded rigor around restricted funds, board reporting, and IRS compliance — and they needed it communicated clearly to boards made up of volunteers, not accountants. That discipline made the firm better at translating financial complexity into plain language for every client.

### Startups and Growing Businesses
icon: TrendingUp

Early-stage companies needed a firm that could grow with them — from initial entity setup to virtual CFO-level guidance as revenue scaled. These clients made clear that compliance alone isn't enough when the stakes are high and the decisions are moving fast.

<!-- block: content-prose -->

## What We Believe About Accounting

Here's the honest version: most accounting firms are built around the calendar, not the client. Tax season dictates the rhythm, and everything else — your questions in August, your hiring decision in October, your refinancing in January — gets squeezed into the margins.

Korbey Lague PLLP operates on a different premise. A CPA should function as a year-round advisor. Full stop. That means being reachable when the decision is live, not filing it away for the next appointment. It means knowing your business well enough to flag a problem before it becomes expensive. It means Ron Lague's AICPA Personal Financial Specialist credential isn't a wall decoration — it's a resource that shapes how the firm thinks about the intersection of business finances and personal wealth.

Compliance is the floor, not the ceiling. Every client deserves a firm that sets its sights higher than a correctly filed return.

<!-- block: team-grid | variant: 3-col -->

## The Team Behind the Work

Credentials matter. So does the fact that these are real people who answer when you call.

### Kelsey Korbey, CPA
Managing Partner

Kelsey co-founded the firm on a conviction that clients deserve year-round attention. Her CPA background anchors the firm's technical work, and her direct communication style means clients always know where they stand.

### Ron Lague, CPA, PFS
Managing Partner

Ron holds both his CPA license and the AICPA's Personal Financial Specialist designation — one of the most rigorous credentials in the profession. That combination shapes how Korbey Lague approaches clients whose business finances and personal financial goals are inseparable.

### Jackie Estes, MBA
Senior Advisor

Jackie brings MBA-level business thinking to client engagements, particularly in areas like cash flow planning, financial reporting, and operational finance. She's the kind of advisor who reads a P&L the way most people read a story.

### Mike Riordan, MBA
Senior Advisor

Mike's MBA background runs straight through his client work — whether that's helping a contractor think through a growth decision or helping a startup build financial infrastructure that can actually scale. Practical is his default mode.

### Richard DelGaudio, CPA
Senior Accountant

Richard's CPA credentials and attention to technical accuracy keep the firm's compliance work sharp. Clients who've worked with firms where the detail work gets delegated down will notice the difference.

<!-- block: cta-banner | variant: image-bg | image: cpa-team-consultation.jpg | query: "professional accountant client consultation office" -->

## What's Next for Korbey Lague PLLP

The firm isn't chasing growth for its own sake. The goal has always been to go deeper — more useful to each client, more embedded in Tyngsborough, more capable of delivering the kind of year-round advisory work that actually changes outcomes.

That means continuing to invest in tools like Sage Intacct, expanding the virtual CFO offering to more businesses that need CFO-level thinking without a full-time hire, and staying grounded in the community that gave the firm its start.

If you're looking for a firm that shows up in July — not just April — the conversation starts here. [Schedule a consultation](/contact)

---

### Our Team
URL: /about/our-team

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->
## The People Behind the Numbers

Most accounting firms introduce themselves in April and disappear by May. Korbey Lague PLLP works differently. Clients here work with the same people year-round — people who know their business structure, their goals for next quarter, and their name when they call. This is a firm built on real relationships, not a revolving door of seasonal preparers. Every person on this team is here because they make that model work.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-left | image: kelsey-korbey-cpa-office.jpg | query: "CPA partner reviewing client financials office" -->
## Kelsey Korbey, CPA — Founder & Managing CPA

Kelsey Korbey built this firm on a simple frustration: too many small business owners were getting tax returns and nothing else. A plan delivered in February, signed in April, and forgotten by June isn't advisory work — it's compliance. Kelsey set out to build something different in Tyngsborough.

A licensed CPA with deep roots in the Massachusetts market, Kelsey has spent her career working with business owners who needed more than a preparer — they needed someone who could read the numbers and tell them what to do next. That instinct shapes how the entire firm operates. The year-round advisory model, the tiered bookkeeping packages, the virtual CFO offering — all of it traces back to her conviction that clients deserve access to real financial thinking, not just at tax time.

Kelsey's client work spans closely held businesses, healthcare practices, and contractors — industries where cash flow timing, entity structure, and owner compensation decisions have real consequences. She brings the same directness to every engagement: here's what the numbers say, here's what it means, here's what we should do about it.

If you've ever wished your accountant would just tell you the truth and help you act on it, that's the practice Kelsey built.

<!-- block: team-grid | variant: 3-col -->
## Our Advisory & Accounting Staff

The team at Korbey Lague brings credentials that matter — CPAs, MBAs, and a Personal Financial Specialist on staff — plus real-world experience in the industries clients actually work in.

### Ron Lague, CPA, PFS
Ron holds both a CPA license and the Personal Financial Specialist (PFS) credential — an AICPA designation awarded only to CPAs who demonstrate advanced proficiency in personal financial planning. That combination matters for clients whose business finances and personal finances are tightly linked: contractors managing equipment debt and retirement accounts, practice owners weighing buy-sell agreements, or business owners planning an exit. Ron brings tax strategy and financial planning into the same conversation, so nothing falls through the gap between the two.

### Jackie Estes, MBA
Jackie's MBA gives her a strong grounding in business operations — she approaches client work at the intersection of accounting and strategy. She's particularly effective with clients who are scaling, restructuring, or trying to make sense of financials that have gotten ahead of their management systems. Jackie is often the person clients hear from when a question goes beyond the numbers on the page.

### Mike Riordan, MBA
Mike's background combines accounting discipline with the broader business perspective his MBA brings to client engagements. He's built strong working knowledge in areas where financial decisions have operational consequences — including contractor businesses and project-based work. Clients describe him as someone who explains things clearly without talking down to them.

### Richard DelGaudio, CPA
Richard is a licensed CPA with experience across tax compliance, financial reporting, and small business advisory work. He's precise where precision is required and practical where it isn't — a balance that serves clients well during both tax season and the quieter months when planning work happens. His presence on the team means clients have another credentialed set of eyes on anything that matters.

<!-- block: content-prose -->
## How We Work With You

At Korbey Lague, clients don't get passed around. When you come on as a client, you have a consistent point of contact — someone who knows your file, your history, and what you're working toward. That person coordinates with the rest of the team as needed, but the relationship stays consistent.

Communication doesn't stop on April 16th. Clients receive proactive outreach when tax law changes affect their situation, when quarterly estimates are due, and when something in their financials warrants a conversation. The team uses Sage Intacct for clients on bookkeeping and virtual CFO engagements, which means real-time visibility into financials — not a monthly PDF dropped in an inbox.

If you have a question in July, someone here will answer it.

<!-- block: cta-banner | variant: image-bg | image: team-consultation-cpa-firm.jpg | query: "professional team meeting small business consultation" -->
## Ready to Work With a Team That's Here Year-Round?

The best time to talk to an accountant isn't April. It's now — before the decisions that affect your taxes, your cash flow, and your financial future are already made. The Korbey Lague team is taking new clients and would like to understand your situation before offering any advice.

That's how good advisory relationships start.

[Schedule a consultation](/contact)

## Services

### Advisory & Virtual CFO
URL: /services/virtual-cfo-advisory

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->

## More Than a Tax Preparer — A Financial Partner in Your Corner

Most business owners see their CPA once a year — usually in a scramble before April 15. The return gets filed, the bill gets paid, and the relationship goes quiet until next January. That's compliance. It's necessary. But it's not strategy.

Korbey Lague PLLP was built on a different premise: that the decisions shaping your business happen in July, not April. When you're weighing a new hire, negotiating a lease, or wondering why cash feels tight despite strong revenue — that's when you need a financial voice in the room.

A Virtual CFO gives you exactly that. CFO-level insight, financial discipline, and strategic clarity — without the cost of a full-time executive. It's the service that turns your CPA relationship from a once-a-year obligation into a year-round advantage.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: cfo-strategy-meeting.jpg | query: "business advisor financial planning client meeting" -->

## What a Virtual CFO Actually Does for Your Business

The title sounds impressive. Here's what it means in practice.

A Virtual CFO from Korbey Lague PLLP works alongside you to build the financial infrastructure your business needs to grow with intention — not just react to whatever the month throws at you. That means cash flow forecasting so you can see problems before they become crises. It means budgeting that reflects your actual business model, not a generic spreadsheet template. It means KPI tracking built around the numbers that actually matter to your industry and your goals.

On the reporting side, you'll receive financial statements that are readable and explained — not just delivered as a PDF attachment and left for you to interpret. Korbey Lague uses Sage Intacct to provide clients with real-time financial visibility, which means your data is current when it counts.

Strategic planning is where this service earns its keep. Whether you're evaluating an acquisition, preparing for a bank loan, modeling the cost of a new service line, or planning an ownership transition, a Virtual CFO brings the financial rigor to stress-test decisions before you make them. The goal is straightforward: fewer surprises, better decisions, a clearer picture of where your business is headed.

<!-- block: industry-cards | variant: 3-col -->

## Who This Service Is Built For

Virtual CFO support isn't one-size-fits-all. Korbey Lague PLLP has built specific experience across five industries — and each one has distinct reasons why ongoing advisory support pays for itself.

### Healthcare Professionals
icon: Stethoscope

Practice owners carry layered financial complexity: insurance reimbursements, staffing overhead, equipment purchases, and compliance requirements that shift constantly. A Virtual CFO brings structure to revenue cycle management, helps with compensation planning, and keeps your practice financially healthy — not just clinically excellent.

### Contractors and Trades
icon: Hammer

Job costing, bonding capacity, equipment financing, and cash flow gaps between project milestones — these are the financial realities contractors live with. Advisory support helps you bid smarter, manage working capital through slow months, and build the financial track record that opens doors with lenders.

### Nonprofits
icon: ShieldCheck

Boards want clean reporting. Funders want accountability. Leadership wants to know the programs are sustainable. A Virtual CFO helps nonprofits maintain audit-ready books, develop realistic budgets tied to funding cycles, and translate financial performance into the kind of reporting that builds donor confidence.

### Service-Based Businesses
icon: Briefcase

Consultants, agencies, and professional services firms often grow faster than their financial systems can handle. A Virtual CFO installs the reporting infrastructure — revenue recognition, utilization tracking, profitability by client or engagement — so growth doesn't create chaos.

### Startups
icon: Zap

Runway modeling, investor-ready financials, pricing strategy, and the decision of when to bring on your first full-time finance hire — these are the questions that define a startup's trajectory. CFO-level guidance early catches expensive assumptions before they're baked into the model.

<!-- block: checklist-section | variant: with-image | image: business-owner-reviewing-financials.jpg | query: "small business owner reviewing financial reports desk" -->

## Signs You've Outgrown Tax-Only Support

Some business inflection points are obvious. Others quietly signal that the annual CPA call is no longer enough.

You've outgrown tax-only support if:

- Cash flow feels unpredictable — revenue is up but you're still waiting on money
- A major decision is coming — a new hire, a location, an equipment purchase — and you don't have a financial model to back it up
- A lender or investor is asking for projections, forecasts, or multi-year financials
- You're not sure which of your services or clients is actually profitable
- Growth is happening faster than your systems can track it
- You've received a tax bill that surprised you — because no one was looking ahead
- You have a bookkeeper but no one is interpreting what the numbers mean
- You've written the phrase "I'll figure it out later" about something financial

If more than two of these land, the conversation is worth having.

<!-- block: process-steps | variant: vertical -->

## How We Work With You — The Korbey Lague Approach

Advisory relationships at Korbey Lague PLLP are structured — not open-ended. Here's what working together actually looks like.

### Discovery and Baseline
Every engagement starts with a clear-eyed look at where you are: current financials, existing systems, immediate pain points, and your goals for the next twelve to thirty-six months. No assumptions.

### Regular Advisory Meetings
Depending on the engagement, clients meet with their advisory team monthly or quarterly. These aren't status calls — they're working sessions built around your financials, upcoming decisions, and anything that's changed since last time.

### Deliverables You Can Use
You'll receive financial reports written for decision-making, not just recordkeeping. Cash flow forecasts, budget-vs-actual comparisons, scenario models, and KPI dashboards — formatted so they're readable without a finance degree.

### Real-Time Visibility Through Sage Intacct
Korbey Lague uses Sage Intacct for clients who need enterprise-grade financial visibility. That means current data, not month-old reports, and a system that scales with your business.

### The Team Behind It
Your advisory engagement draws on the credentials of the full firm. Ron Lague, CPA, PFS — one of a limited number of CPAs nationally to hold the AICPA's Personal Financial Specialist designation — leads strategic advisory work. The broader team includes Kelsey Korbey, CPA; Richard DelGaudio, CPA; and MBA-credentialed professionals Jackie Estes and Mike Riordan. You're not getting a junior associate with a checklist. You're getting the firm.

<!-- block: cta-banner | variant: image-bg | image: cfo-consultation-cta.jpg | query: "professional business consultation handshake office" -->

## Ready to Put a CFO-Level Strategist on Your Team?

You've built something worth protecting — and growing. The decisions you're facing right now deserve more than a once-a-year conversation.

Korbey Lague PLLP works with business owners in Tyngsborough and across Massachusetts who are ready to trade financial uncertainty for financial clarity. If you've been wondering whether a Virtual CFO is right for your stage, start with a conversation.

[Schedule a consultation](/contact)

---

### Tax
URL: /services/tax

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->
## More Than a Tax Return — A Tax Strategy

A tax return tells you what happened. A tax strategy changes what happens next.

At Korbey Lague PLLP, tax preparation is the starting point — not the finish line. Too many business owners and individuals file their return in April and don't think about taxes again until the following March. That gap is where money gets left behind. Our CPAs work year-round, which means we're identifying deductions, flagging planning opportunities, and watching for law changes in July just as intently as we are in February. The result isn't just a completed return — it's a tax position you understand and one that's been built deliberately, not assembled in a rush.

<!-- block: service-cards | variant: 3-col -->
## Tax Services We Provide

From a straightforward individual return to a complex business filing with multiple entities, we handle the full range.

### Individual Tax Returns
icon: FileCheck

Federal and Massachusetts state returns prepared by licensed CPAs — not seasonal staff. Includes W-2 earners, self-employed filers, rental income, investment activity, and more.

### Business Tax Returns
icon: Briefcase

Returns for S-corps, C-corps, partnerships, LLCs, and sole proprietors. We prepare your return with full context for your business — not as an isolated document.

### Quarterly Estimated Taxes
icon: Calendar

Missed or underpaid estimates cost you at filing. We calculate accurate quarterly payments and keep you on schedule so there are no surprises in April.

### Tax Planning
icon: ChartLine

Proactive strategy sessions — entity structure reviews, retirement contribution timing, capital gains planning, depreciation elections, and more — done before year-end when changes can still matter.

### IRS Representation
icon: ShieldCheck

Receiving IRS correspondence is stressful. Our CPAs handle notices, audits, and correspondence directly on your behalf, so you're not dealing with the IRS alone.

### Multi-Year Filings
icon: FileText

Behind on filings? We handle multi-year catch-up returns without judgment — and with a clear plan to get you current and compliant.

<!-- block: industry-cards | variant: 3-col -->
## Who We Work With

Different industries carry different tax burdens. Here's why the clients we serve specifically benefit from working with a firm that knows their world.

### Healthcare Professionals
icon: Stethoscope

Physicians, dentists, and practice owners face layered tax complexity — practice entity structure, S-corp compensation ratios, retirement plan selection, and personal income that often pushes into the top federal brackets. A generalist misses the details that matter here.

### Contractors & Trades
icon: Hammer

Job costing, equipment depreciation under Section 179 and bonus depreciation rules, subcontractor 1099 requirements, and the home office deduction — contractors carry a tax profile that looks nothing like a typical W-2 employee's. We know the difference.

### Nonprofits
icon: Users

Form 990 preparation, unrelated business income tax, and compliance with state and federal requirements are not optional for nonprofits. We prepare accurate filings that protect your organization's tax-exempt status.

### Service-Based Businesses
icon: Building

Consultants, agencies, and professional service firms often qualify for the Section 199A qualified business income deduction — but the rules are specific and phase-outs apply. Knowing whether you qualify, and by how much, takes more than a checkbox.

### Startups
icon: Lightbulb

Entity selection at formation has tax consequences that follow you for years. Startups working with Korbey Lague get that conversation before filing the first return — not after the wrong structure is already locked in.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: seasonal-tax-firm-contrast.jpg | query: "accountant reviewing paperwork year round office" -->
## The Problem With Seasonal Tax Firms

If the only time you hear from your CPA is when they need your W-2s, that's not an advisory relationship — it's a transaction.

The problem isn't that seasonal preparers file bad returns. The problem is that a return prepared in March reflects decisions made in the prior year that can no longer be changed. By the time a seasonal firm looks at your file, the window for meaningful tax strategy has already closed. Retirement contributions could have been larger. An asset purchase could have been timed better. An entity election could have saved thousands.

Korbey Lague is still working in July. That's not a marketing line — it's what makes the difference between filing a return and actually controlling your tax outcome.

<!-- block: process-steps | variant: horizontal -->
## What the Tax Process Looks Like With Us

Knowing what to expect removes most of the friction. Here's how a tax engagement works from start to finish.

### Initial Consultation
We start by understanding your situation — business structure, prior year filings, pain points, and any open questions. This is where we catch what other firms have missed.

### Document Gathering
We provide a clear, organized checklist so you know exactly what to pull together. No guessing, no back-and-forth asking for the same thing twice.

### Preparation
A credentialed CPA prepares your return — not a staff preparer working a seasonal queue. Every return goes through an internal review before it leaves our office.

### Review & Questions
Before filing, we walk you through the return. You should understand what's on it and why.

### Filing
Federal, state, and any applicable local filings are submitted. You receive copies and confirmation.

### Year-Round Check-Ins
The engagement doesn't end at filing. Quarterly reviews, year-end planning conversations, and availability when something changes in your business keep your tax position current all year.

<!-- block: cta-banner | variant: image-bg | image: tax-consultation-cta.jpg | query: "CPA client consultation professional office" -->
## Ready to Stop Dreading Tax Season?

Tax doesn't have to be something you brace for. When a firm is engaged with your finances year-round, April becomes a formality — not a fire drill.

Korbey Lague PLLP serves individuals and businesses in Tyngsborough, Massachusetts and the surrounding region with tax preparation, planning, and advisory services handled by credentialed CPAs — including Ron Lague, CPA, PFS and Kelsey Korbey, CPA.

[Schedule a consultation](/contact)

---

### Bookkeeping & Payroll
URL: /services/bookkeeping-payroll

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->
## More Than Recordkeeping — A Financial Foundation You Can Build On

Most business owners know their books matter. What gets lost is *why* they matter — and what becomes possible when they're actually right.

Messy books aren't just an accounting problem. They're a tax planning problem. A cash flow problem. A "can I get this loan" problem. At Korbey Lague PLLP, bookkeeping and payroll aren't back-office chores we hand off to a junior staffer. They're the foundation every other financial conversation is built on. We treat them that way year-round — not just when April rolls around.

---

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: small-business-owner-reviewing-financials.jpg | query: "small business owner reviewing financial reports desk" -->
## Bookkeeping Services Designed Around Your Business

If you're reconciling accounts yourself at midnight or trusting spreadsheets held together with hope, you already know something's off. The question is whether you're catching the problems before they cost you.

Korbey Lague's bookkeeping services cover the full cycle — transaction categorization, bank and credit card reconciliations, accounts receivable and payable tracking, and monthly or quarterly financial reporting. For businesses inheriting a backlog, we also offer cleanup and catch-up bookkeeping to get the ledger current before moving forward.

The emphasis is on accuracy and timeliness. A financial statement that's three months stale isn't a financial statement — it's a history lesson. When your books close on time, with the right numbers, you can make decisions based on where your business actually is, not where it was.

Our team includes credentialed CPAs — including Kelsey Korbey, CPA and Richard DelGaudio, CPA — alongside team members with MBAs, so the people touching your books understand not just the entries, but the business logic behind them.

---

<!-- block: checklist-section | variant: with-image | image: payroll-processing-office.jpg | query: "payroll processing business office professional" -->
## Payroll That Runs On Time, Every Time

Payroll errors are expensive in ways that compound fast. A misclassified worker triggers IRS scrutiny. A late deposit means penalties. An incorrectly filed 941 means amended returns. For businesses in healthcare, construction, or skilled trades — where your workforce is your product — a payroll problem isn't a back-office inconvenience. It's a staffing crisis waiting to happen.

Korbey Lague handles payroll processing from end to end:

- Accurate wage calculations for hourly, salaried, and tipped employees
- Federal, state, and local tax withholding and remittance
- Direct deposit processing
- Quarterly and annual compliance filings (941, 940, W-2s, 1099s)
- New hire reporting
- Garnishment and deduction management
- Year-end payroll reconciliation

The goal is a payroll run your employees never have to question. When staff trust that their check is right and on time, you spend less time fielding complaints and more time running the business.

---

<!-- block: industry-cards | variant: 3-col -->
## Who We Serve With These Services

### Healthcare Professionals
icon: Stethoscope

Practice owners — physicians, dentists, therapists — deal with a mix of W-2 staff and 1099 contractors, plus billing cycles that don't map neatly to calendar months. Clean books and compliant payroll aren't optional when licensing, credentialing, and insurance reimbursement are all tied to your financial standing.

### Contractors and Trades
icon: Hammer

Job costing requires clean categorization — material costs, labor, subcontractors, and equipment all need to land in the right bucket. Misclassifying a subcontractor or falling behind on quarterly payroll taxes can put a bonding or licensing renewal at risk faster than most owners expect.

### Nonprofits
icon: HeartPulse

Fund accounting requires a different structure than standard business bookkeeping. Grants, restricted funds, and board-level reporting all demand that the books be segmented, current, and audit-ready — not patched together at fiscal year-end.

### Service-Based Businesses
icon: Briefcase

Consultancies, agencies, and professional service firms often struggle with revenue timing — retainers, project billing, and deferred income create reconciliation complexity that generic bookkeeping software doesn't handle well on its own. Having a CPA firm in your corner means those issues get caught early.

### Startups
icon: TrendingUp

Early-stage companies often get bookkeeping wrong in ways that haunt them later — investor due diligence, SBA loan applications, and R&D tax credit documentation all require clean historical records. Starting the right way costs far less than reconstructing two years of transactions.

---

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-left | image: accountant-advising-client-meeting.jpg | query: "accountant advising small business client meeting" -->
## What Happens When Your Books Are Actually Clean

Accurate books aren't the end goal — they're the starting line.

When your financials are current and correct, your CPA can look at tax positioning before December, not after. A lender reviewing your loan application sees organized, reconciled statements — not a shoebox of receipts. Cash flow conversations shift from guesswork to data. And when you're ready to talk growth — hiring, equipment, expansion — those decisions are grounded in numbers you can trust.

This is the connection that separates a bookkeeping service from a bookkeeping *strategy*. At Korbey Lague, clean books feed directly into the tax planning, virtual CFO, and advisory work our team does year-round. The services aren't siloed — they build on each other.

Ron Lague, CPA, PFS, holds the Personal Financial Specialist designation from the AICPA — the only CPA credential specifically tied to personal financial planning. When your business financials are organized, those conversations extend further.

---

## Why Tyngsborough Business Owners Choose Korbey Lague

National bookkeeping platforms offer software. Seasonal-only CPAs offer a filing. Neither is the same as a local firm that knows your business in March, in July, and in November.

Korbey Lague PLLP is based in Tyngsborough and built around year-round availability. The team — credentialed CPAs, MBAs, and a PFS — is reachable when something changes mid-year, not just when a deadline forces a conversation. That kind of access is what business owners here have come to expect from us, and it's what we deliver.

### Financial Reporting
URL: /services/financial-reporting

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->
## More Than Numbers on a Page

Most business owners get a set of financial statements once a year and spend the next ten minutes trying to figure out what they're actually looking at. The numbers are there. The context isn't.

Korbey Lague PLLP prepares financial reports that you can read, act on, and hand to a banker or board member with confidence. That's a different thing than a packet handed over in April and not touched again until next spring.

Financial reporting done right gives you a live picture of where your business stands — not a historical artifact. Whether you need compiled statements for a lender, reviewed financials for a board presentation, or monthly management reports that actually help you run the business, we produce work that holds up to scrutiny. And because we're a year-round firm, not a seasonal one, those reports are available when the real decisions happen — in July, in October, whenever the moment requires them.

<!-- block: checklist-section | variant: with-image | image: financial-statements-review.jpg | query: "accountant reviewing financial statements client meeting" -->
## What Our Financial Reporting Services Include

Financial reporting covers a range of deliverables, and what you need depends on who's asking and why. Here's what Korbey Lague prepares:

- **Compiled Financial Statements** — CPA-prepared statements that organize your financial data into a standard format, without expressing an opinion on accuracy. Required by many lenders and third parties.
- **Reviewed Financial Statements** — A step above compilation: we perform analytical procedures and inquiries to provide limited assurance that your financials conform to generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP).
- **Prepared Financial Statements** — Management-use statements that give you a clear, organized view of your financials without the formality of a compilation or review engagement.
- **Income Statements (Profit & Loss)** — A clear breakdown of revenue, cost of goods sold, operating expenses, and net income over a specific period.
- **Balance Sheets** — A point-in-time snapshot of what your business owns, owes, and the equity held by owners.
- **Cash Flow Statements** — Where did the cash come from, and where did it go? This report answers the question your income statement can't.
- **Management Reports** — Custom internal reports built around how you actually run your business — by job, by department, by location, or however you need to see it.

Every report is prepared by credentialed professionals — including CPAs Kelsey Korbey, Ron Lague, and Richard DelGaudio — and reviewed for accuracy before it leaves the office.

<!-- block: industry-cards | variant: 3-col -->
## Who We Prepare Financial Reports For

Korbey Lague works across five primary industries, and the financial reporting needs in each one are specific — not interchangeable.

### Healthcare Professionals
icon: Stethoscope

Physicians, dentists, and practice owners often operate across multiple revenue streams — fee-for-service, insurance reimbursements, ancillary income. Accurate financial reporting separates practice profitability from personal draw and gives you the documentation you need for practice financing or partnership buyouts.

### Contractors and Trades
icon: Hammer

For general contractors and subcontractors, financial statements aren't just internal documents. Bonding companies require CPA-prepared financials to evaluate your capacity. So do construction lenders. Job costing reports tied to your financial statements also show where individual projects are making or losing money.

### Nonprofits
icon: Building2

Grant funders, state agencies, and boards of directors all require accurate, current financials — and many grant agreements specify the form they must take. Reviewed or compiled statements prepared by a CPA protect your grant compliance and support your annual audit process.

### Service-Based Businesses
icon: Briefcase

Consultants, agencies, and professional service firms often struggle to connect their financial statements to real operational decisions. Monthly management reports tied to your billing cycle and overhead give you information you can act on — not just year-end summaries.

### Startups
icon: Lightbulb

Early-stage companies raising capital need financials that investors can trust. Korbey Lague prepares startup financials that meet investor expectations and support due diligence — including the cash flow statements that matter most when runway is the question on everyone's mind.

<!-- block: content-prose -->
## Why Accurate Financial Reporting Matters Year-Round

Financial reporting isn't a once-a-year obligation. It's a tool — and like any tool, it only works when you use it at the right moment.

Consider what actually requires current, accurate financials: applying for a business line of credit, bringing on a new partner, buying out an existing one, preparing for a bank audit, responding to an IRS inquiry, or deciding whether to hire your next employee. None of those moments are scheduled in April. They happen when they happen.

Korbey Lague has built its Tyngsborough practice around year-round availability for exactly this reason. When your banker calls and needs updated statements by Friday, we can respond — because we're not in the middle of tax season, and because your books are already current. When your board wants quarterly financials for the next meeting, they exist.

The firms that hand you a packet in April and disappear until March can't do that. We can.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: banker-loan-meeting.jpg | query: "business owner meeting with banker loan documents" -->
## Reports Your Banker, Board, or Bonding Company Will Trust

Lenders, surety companies, boards of directors, and outside investors all have one thing in common: they want financial statements prepared by a CPA, not self-prepared by a business owner in QuickBooks.

The reason isn't procedural. It's risk. A third party extending credit or approving a bond is relying on the accuracy of what you present. CPA-prepared statements — particularly compiled or reviewed engagements — carry professional accountability behind them. That accountability is what makes them credible to outside parties.

Korbey Lague produces financial reports that hold up when someone actually looks at them. Ron Lague, CPA, PFS, and the firm's other credentialed professionals follow AICPA standards on every engagement. When your bank asks for three years of financials, or your bonding agent needs a reviewed statement before they'll extend capacity, you hand them work that won't create more questions than it answers.

If your current statements were self-prepared or haven't been touched since last year's tax return, that's worth fixing before the next request comes in.

<!-- block: cta-banner | variant: image-bg | image: financial-clarity-consultation.jpg | query: "professional handshake financial consultation office" -->
## Ready to See the Real Health of Your Business?

If your financial statements are hard to read, out of date, or unlikely to satisfy a lender's request, this is a straightforward problem to solve. Korbey Lague PLLP works with business owners in Tyngsborough and across Massachusetts to produce financial reports that are accurate, current, and built for the decisions you actually face.

The first step is a conversation — not a commitment.

[Schedule a consultation](/contact)

### Nonprofit Accounting
URL: /industries/nonprofits

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->
## More Than Compliance — A Partner Who Understands Your Mission

Most accounting firms show up in March and disappear in May. That's not how nonprofit finance works — and it's not how Korbey Lague PLLP works.

Your board meets in October. Your grant deadline is in February. Your auditors want documentation you weren't sure to keep. The 990 is the smallest part of what a nonprofit finance team actually manages, and if your CPA only calls when it's time to file, you're carrying those pressures alone.

Korbey Lague PLLP works with nonprofits in the Merrimack Valley and greater Tyngsborough area as a year-round resource — not a once-a-year vendor. That means your executive director has someone to call when a funder asks for a financial summary by Thursday. It means your board treasurer isn't guessing at restricted fund balances before the annual meeting. And it means your organization's finances tell the right story to donors, grantors, and the IRS — because they're being managed with that outcome in mind, all year long.

---

<!-- block: service-cards | variant: 3-col -->
## Nonprofit Accounting Services We Provide

### Form 990 Preparation
icon: FileCheck

Accurate, complete 990 filing that reflects your organization's programs, financials, and governance honestly — and holds up to scrutiny from the IRS or curious donors researching your GuideStar profile.

### Fund Accounting & Bookkeeping
icon: Coins

Monthly or quarterly bookkeeping built around nonprofit fund structures — tracking restricted, temporarily restricted, and unrestricted funds so your financials are always reconciled and decision-ready.

### Financial Statement Preparation
icon: ChartBar

Statement of financial position, statement of activities, and cash flow reporting prepared in a format that your board can read and your auditors can rely on.

### Grant Tracking & Compliance
icon: ClipboardCheck

Grant-by-grant expense tracking that links expenditures to specific funding sources, so you can produce funder-required reports without a last-minute scramble through your general ledger.

### Audit Support
icon: ShieldCheck

Preparation and coordination support for organizations subject to single audit requirements or independent audits — including document organization, schedule preparation, and auditor liaison.

### Board Financial Reporting
icon: Users

Board-ready financial packages presented in plain language — so your finance committee walks into every meeting understanding where the organization stands, not just what the numbers say.

---

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: nonprofit-finance-challenges.jpg | query: nonprofit executive director reviewing financial documents -->
## The Challenges Nonprofits Face (And How We Solve Them)

Nonprofit finance has a specific kind of pressure that for-profit accounting doesn't. You're accountable to donors, to grantors, to your board, and to the IRS — all at once, often with a small (or overwhelmed) internal team.

Restricted fund management is where many organizations run into trouble first. Spending a restricted grant on the wrong expense category — even accidentally — can trigger compliance issues with funders and complicate your 990. Korbey Lague PLLP tracks fund restrictions at the transaction level, so there's no ambiguity at year-end.

Board reporting pressure is real. Volunteer board members often don't have financial backgrounds, but they're still responsible for fiduciary oversight. Clear, accurate reports prepared on a consistent schedule take that pressure off your executive director and give your board what they actually need to govern well.

Staff turnover in small finance teams creates gaps that don't wait for a good time. When your bookkeeper leaves in June, your grant reports don't pause. As an outside resource, Korbey Lague PLLP provides continuity that doesn't depend on any single employee — your records stay current, your obligations stay met, and your organization keeps moving.

---

### Social Service Organizations
icon: HeartPulse

Community-based nonprofits delivering direct services — housing support, food access, behavioral health programs — where grant compliance and fund tracking are central to operations.

### Foundations & Charitable Trusts
icon: Building2

Private and community foundations that need accurate reporting on grantmaking activity, investment income, and excise tax obligations under Form 990-PF.

### Religious Organizations
icon: Globe

Churches and faith-based nonprofits navigating the intersection of religious exemptions, contribution tracking, and transparent financial reporting to their congregations.

### Trade & Professional Associations
icon: Briefcase

Member-funded organizations that need clear reporting on dues revenue, program expenses, and the line between exempt and non-exempt income.

### Community Nonprofits
icon: MapPin

Smaller organizations in Tyngsborough, Lowell, Chelmsford, and across the Merrimack Valley that need professional-grade financial support without the overhead of a full-time finance staff.

Boards don't govern well with stale financials. Executive directors don't sleep well when grant deadlines are approaching and the numbers aren't reconciled. The firms that actually serve nonprofits well are the ones available when those moments happen — not just the ones who file a clean 990 in May.

Korbey Lague PLLP is based in Tyngsborough, Massachusetts, and built to be a genuine year-round resource. That means returning calls and emails between filing seasons. It means preparing your board financials before the September meeting, not the week after. It means your executive director has a CPA to call when a funding question comes up — not a voicemail box.

The firm's credentials back that up. Ron Lague holds both his CPA license and the AICPA's Personal Financial Specialist (PFS) designation. Kelsey Korbey and Richard DelGaudio are CPAs. Jackie Estes and Mike Riordan hold MBAs. That depth means your nonprofit isn't being handed to a junior staffer — you're working with people who understand how financial decisions connect to organizational outcomes.

Donor transparency and board confidence aren't soft goals. They're what keep your organization funded and functional. The right accounting firm helps you build both.
